_comment: State Teachers Retirement System of Ohio (STRS Ohio) — Defined Benefit, Defined Contribution, and Combined Plans. Consumer-agnostic data only.
systemName: State Teachers Retirement System of Ohio
administeredBy: STRS Ohio — governed by the 11-member State Teachers Retirement Board
boardComposition: 5 elected contributing teacher members, 2 elected retired teacher members, 3 appointed investment experts (by Governor, House Speaker/Senate President jointly, and Treasurer of State), 1 Director of Department of Education and Workforce or designee. By Sept. 1, 2028, the board will consist of three elected contributing teacher members, two elected retired teacher members, and six appointed members (pending litigation — Franklin County Court of Common Pleas issued preliminary injunction in Oct. 2025).

totalMembers.note: ~543,000 total active, inactive, and benefit recipients. First statewide actuarially-based teacher retirement system in the United States. Approximately one retiree for each active teacher (mature plan).

populations: Ohio public school teachers and administrators (preK-12)
Employees of county boards of developmental disabilities
Community and charter school educators covered by statute
coverage.employers: 1,139 school districts, vocational schools, colleges and universities, county boards of developmental disabilities, and community and charter schools
coverage.planChoice: New members have 180 days from first day of paid service to choose from three plans (DB, DC, Combined). Default enrollment is DB Plan if no election made within 180 days. Full-time higher education faculty may also select an Alternative Retirement Plan (ARP) within 120 days.

socialSecurity.note: Ohio public educators do not contribute to or receive Social Security from STRS Ohio-covered employment. Ohio is a non-SS state for public educators. The Social Security Fairness Act (H.R. 82, Jan 2025) eliminated WEP and GPO for SS benefits payable after Dec 2023. SSA began paying retroactive benefits and increasing monthly payments in Feb. 2025.

compensationLimits.note: Maximum salary used for benefit calculations and contributions. Based on IRS Section 401(a)(17) limits. DC Plan: contributions stop when employee + employer contributions total $70,000 for the calendar year.

plans.overview: STRS Ohio offers three plan choices: Defined Benefit (DB), Defined Contribution (DC), and Combined. Members selecting DC or Combined have a one-time option to reselect their plan before completing the fifth year of membership.

formula.structure: 2.2% x Years of Service Credit x Final Average Salary

finalAverageSalary.note: Average of the member's five highest years of Ohio public earnings. If contributions were made for fewer years than the FAS period, total compensation is divided by total years of service credit.

formula.exampleCalculation: 30 years x $70,000 FAS x 2.2% = $46,200 annual / $3,850 monthly

retirementEligibility.overview: Eligibility requirements vary by retirement window due to the Sustainable Benefit Plan (SBP) framework. Requirements change in 2030 and 2032 as the system phases toward its long-term standard.

unreduced.note: 32 years of service at any age

reduced.note: Reduced benefit at 27 years at any age
Eligible at age 60 with 5 years even if unreduced standard not met

windows.note: Long-term standard established by SBP framework. Board goal is to make 32-year unreduced the permanent standard, subject to actuarial approval.

benefitCalculationTable.description: Percentage of Final Average Salary payable as a Single Life Annuity, by years of Ohio service credit and age at retirement. For unreduced benefits, the factor equals years x 2.2%. For reduced benefits, an actuarial reduction is applied. This table is for the June 2025 through May 2030 eligibility window. The board periodically improves reduction factors under the SBP.

unreducedBenchmarks.note: At unreduced retirement, benefit = years x 2.2% of FAS with no reduction.

reducedBenefitFactors.note: For reduced early retirement, the benefit calculation table combines the 2.2% multiplier and actuarial reduction into a single percentage-of-FAS factor for each age/service combination. Selected data points from the official benefit calculation table (effective June 2025-May 2030 window):
reducedBenefitFactors.reductionPattern: Approximately 9.1% reduction at age 60 vs age 65. Additional ~1.7% per year below age 60. Exact factors available from STRS Ohio's official benefit calculation table or Online Personal Account Service Retirement calculator.

vesting.qualifyingServiceNote: 5 years of qualifying service credit includes: earned credit with STRS Ohio, OPERS, or SERS; restored withdrawn credit from those systems; interrupted Ohio public service due to military service; and credit transferred from OP&F, HPRS, or Cincinnati Retirement System.

member.note: 14% of annual salary. Rate set by Ohio Legislature. Has increased 9 times in 40 years, from 8.7% to 14%. Ohio teachers contribute at the 3rd-highest rate among comparable non-SS teacher pension systems (median 9.25%).

employer.note: 14% of total teacher payroll to Employers' Trust Fund. Ohio employer rate is second-lowest among non-SS teacher pension states (median ~19.6%). STRS Ohio advocates for a legislative increase to the employer rate.

cola.frameworkDescription: Annual process under which the State Teachers Retirement Board and its actuaries evaluate potential benefit changes. COLAs may be granted if the change does not materially impair fiscal integrity of the system per Ohio law.

eligibility.note: New benefit recipients first eligible for COLA on the 5th anniversary of their retirement date.

cola.note: COLAs are simple (non-compounding) — calculated on the base benefit and added to the regular monthly check permanently.

recentCOLAs.eligibility: Retirees who started benefits on June 1, 2022 or earlier
recentCOLAs.authority: State Teachers Retirement Board, March 2026 board meeting

recentCOLAs.eligibility: Retirees who started benefits on June 1, 2021 or earlier
recentCOLAs.authority: State Teachers Retirement Board, April 2025 board meeting

cola.boardGoal: The board's goal is to establish a permanent 1% annual COLA as part of the benefit structure, subject to the SBP framework and actuarial soundness.

sb69.title: SB 69 — Reform the state's public retirement system law
sb69.referredToCommittee: 2025-02-12 — Senate Financial Institutions, Insurance and Technology
sb69.statusDetail: No hearings or substantive action taken. Bill is a placeholder declaring intent to reform all five Ohio public retirement systems. Ohio Senate unlikely to act until after state budget passage (FY2026-27). Legislature in spring recess expected through May 5, 2026 primary election.

sb6_fiduciary.title: SB 6 — Fiduciary duty standards for public pension investments
sb6_fiduciary.statusDetail: Signed by Gov. DeWine in December 2025. Requires fiduciaries to only consider pecuniary factors — those with material effect on financial risk and return — when making investment decisions.

hb96_governance.title: HB 96 — STRS Board governance changes (via state budget)
hb96_governance.statusDetail: Franklin County Court of Common Pleas granted preliminary injunction in Oct. 2025 preventing changes to STRS Board composition from taking effect while under litigation.

cola.authority: Ohio Revised Code 3307.67; Sustainable Benefit Plan framework

plansOfPayment.overview: Members must select a plan of payment at retirement that determines the benefit structure for their lifetime.

options.name: Single Life Annuity (SLA)
options.description: Maximum monthly benefit for member's lifetime. No continuing monthly payments to a survivor. If total payments are less than member contributions, remaining contributions paid to beneficiary.

options.name: Joint and Survivor Annuity
options.description: Reduced monthly benefit during member's lifetime; continuing lifetime monthly payments to one designated beneficiary after member's death. Options include: 100% to beneficiary, 75%, 66-2/3%, 50%, or 25% to beneficiary. Reversion option available (benefit reverts to SLA amount if beneficiary predeceases member). Up to 4 primary beneficiaries may be named.

options.name: Annuity Certain
options.description: Reduced monthly benefit with guaranteed payments for a specified period (5, 10, 15, 20, or 25 years). If member dies during guaranteed period, beneficiary receives remaining payments. Benefits continue for member's lifetime beyond the guaranteed period.

plansOfPayment.spousalConsent: Without spousal consent, member will receive a Joint and Survivor Annuity with 50% to beneficiary (with reversion) and no PLOP payment.

plop.name: Partial Lump Sum Option Payment (PLOP)
plop.description: Members may elect to receive a lump sum at retirement in exchange for a permanently reduced ongoing monthly benefit. The monthly reduction is permanent — future benefit changes (including COLAs) are calculated on the reduced benefit.

range.minimum: 6 times the monthly Single Life Annuity benefit
range.maximum: 36 times the monthly Single Life Annuity benefit
range.floor: Monthly benefit after PLOP cannot be less than 50% of the original monthly amount
range.increments: $1,000 increments (unless minimum or maximum amount is selected)

costExample.description: Example: Member retiring July 1, 2024, at age 60 with $3,000/month SLA benefit, taking $50,000 PLOP
costExample.note: Cost per $1,000 varies by age at retirement. Younger retirees have higher cost per $1,000 due to longer expected payout period.

plop.timing: PLOP payment is paid no earlier than 91 days after retirement date or the date all necessary information is received and benefit is finalized, whichever is later.

taxImplications.federalWithholding: 20% mandatory federal tax withholding on payments not directly rolled over
taxImplications.earlyWithdrawalPenalty: 10% penalty may apply if received before age 59-1/2, unless separated from service and taken in/after the year member turned age 55
taxImplications.rollover: May be rolled over to IRA or qualified plan to defer taxation. Rollover must be completed within 60 days if paid directly to member.
taxImplications.ohioTax: Subject to Ohio income tax for Ohio residents. Exempt from Ohio local/municipal taxes but may be subject to school district income tax.
taxImplications.rmd: Members age 73+ may be subject to IRS required minimum distribution rules and may not roll over the entire PLOP amount.

programs.name: Disability Retirement
programs.eligibility: 5 years qualifying service credit (existing members on June 30, 2013) or 10 years (members on or after July 1, 2013); must file within 2 years of last contributing service; under age 60 at filing; have most service credit with STRS Ohio vs OPERS/SERS

programs.name: Disability Allowance
programs.note: Separate program — details available from STRS Ohio.

survivorBenefits.description: Monthly income to qualified family members of the deceased member. Benefit amount depends on years of service and survivor relationship.

deathBenefits.note: Service retirement and disability benefit recipients may purchase an additional optional death benefit of $1,000 or $2,000, funded by deductions from monthly benefit.

healthCareCoverage.overview: Optional health care coverage for eligible benefit recipients and dependents. STRS Ohio subsidizes individual monthly premiums for eligible recipients. Coverage not guaranteed — subject to change by board action.

aetnaMedicarePlan.eligibility: Medicare enrollees (Medicare Parts A & B required at age 65 or when eligible)
aetnaMedicarePlan.features: Lower premiums and out-of-pocket costs than Aetna Basic. Includes hospital, medical, prescription, fitness, vision, and hearing.
aetnaMedicarePlan.prescriptionAdmin: SilverScript (Medicare Part D plan, CVS affiliate)

aetnaBasicPlan.eligibility: Available to all enrollees. Only option for non-Medicare enrollees.

retireOnOrAfter_2023_08_01.note: At least 20 years of service credit required to qualify for coverage and a subsidy. 30+ years for maximum subsidy.

retire_2004_01_01_to_2023_07_01.note: At least 15 years of service credit required to qualify for coverage and a subsidy.

retireBefore_2004_01_01.note: No minimum years for coverage; at least 15 years for premium subsidy.

eligibilityByRetirementDate.subsidyAlignmentNote: Board aligned health care premium subsidy eligibility to pension eligibility in May 2025. For retirements Aug. 1, 2023 through July 1, 2032, 30 years for maximum subsidy. After Aug. 1, 2032, maximum subsidy aligns with unreduced pension eligibility.

premiums_2026.note: Monthly premiums reduced by subsidy based on years of service. Lower years = higher premium. Premiums shown are for benefit recipients eligible for subsidy.

medicarePlan_aetna.description: Aetna Medicare Plan (Medicare Advantage PPO). Premiums include $1 charge for CMS Medicare Prescription Payment Plan (M3P). Reduced by $30 Medicare Part B credit for enrollees in an STRS Ohio Medicare plan.
medicarePlan_aetna.note: Additional tiers exist down to 15/20 years with higher premiums. Null indicates retirement cohort is not eligible at that service level.

nonMedicarePlan_aetnaBasic.description: Aetna Basic Plan (PPO or Indemnity) for non-Medicare enrollees.

medicarePlan_aetnaBasic.description: Aetna Basic Plan for Medicare enrollees who choose it instead of Medicare Advantage.

healthCareAssistanceProgram.note: Benefit recipients enrolled in the Health Care Assistance Program pay $0 monthly premium. Eligible dependents pay premiums shown in standard tiers.

dentalAndVision.enrollment: Every two years during open enrollment (even years). 2025-2026 premiums continue from prior enrollment.
dentalAndVision.note: Dental and vision plans available at additional cost to benefit recipients and eligible dependents.

healthCareCoverage.medicareRequirement: All medical plan participants must enroll in Medicare Parts A & B at age 65 or when eligible. Declining Medicare Part B or premium-free Part A makes member ineligible for STRS Ohio medical coverage.

mitigatingRate.note: A portion (currently 2.91%) of the employer's 14% contribution goes to STRS Ohio as a mitigating rate per Ohio Revised Code 3305.06 to fund the unfunded liability. Subject to change; HB 520 set 4.5% max with review every 5 years.

definedContribution.vestingSchedule: 20% per year of employer contributions; fully vested after 5 years. Member contributions immediately vested.
definedContribution.contributionCap: $70,000 combined employee + employer contributions per calendar year
definedContribution.portability: Account balance can be rolled over to IRA, eligible employer plan, or other qualified plan upon leaving STRS Ohio-covered employment.
definedContribution.reselectionOption: One-time option to reselect plan before completing fifth year of membership.

combinedPlan.overview: Hybrid plan combining Defined Benefit and Defined Contribution components. Provides survivor/disability benefits and health care eligibility like the DB Plan, plus member-directed investment for the DC portion.

member.note: Of the member's 14%, 2% goes to fund the DB benefit and 12% goes to the DC account.

employer.note: All 14% employer contribution funds the DB portion.

dbComponent.formula: 1.0% x Years of Service Credit x Final Average Salary
dbComponent.note: DB formula is 1.0% (not 2.2% as in the full DB Plan). Members who retire with 35 years under this formula receive 35% of FAS as an annual DB benefit.
dbComponent.retirementEligibility: Same eligibility windows as the DB Plan (32/33/34 years unreduced, 27/28/29 years reduced, age 60 with 5 years).

dcComponent.description: Account balance from member contributions (12% of salary) and investment gains/losses. At retirement, member may receive as lump sum or monthly annuity (if $25+ per month).
dcComponent.investmentRisk: Assumed entirely by the member for the DC portion.

combinedPlan.reselectionOption: One-time option to reselect plan before completing fifth year of membership.

alternativeRetirementPlan.eligibility: Full-time higher education faculty only. Must elect within 120 days of first day of eligible employment.
alternativeRetirementPlan.note: Not administered by STRS Ohio. Employer contributes to member's selected ARP provider (currently 11.09% of salary, net of mitigating rate to STRS Ohio). No STRS Ohio benefits, health care, or survivor/disability coverage.

fundingStatus.investmentReturn_10yr_ranking: 5th highest among 80+ public pension funds tracked by Pensions & Investments

cashFlow.note: STRS Ohio pays out nearly $4 billion more in benefits than it receives in contributions each year. This mature plan status makes the system more vulnerable to market declines.

healthCareFund.note: Health care fund ratio decreased from 158% to 128.3% driven by higher-than-expected claims and retirement eligibility expansions. Further eligibility expansion will continue to put stress on the Health Care Program.

supplementalRetirement.note: Ohio educators may contribute to 403(b) and 457(b) supplemental retirement plans in addition to STRS Ohio. 2025 limit: $23,500 (under age 50); catch-up provisions available for age 50+.

researchGaps: Complete actuarial reduction factor table (full grid by age 44-65+ and 5-40 years service) — partial data provided; full table behind member login
PLOP cost-per-$1,000 table by age at retirement (only age 60 example available publicly)
Disability allowance program details (separate from disability retirement)
